violations.current_code:
# Step 21: Responsive verification (viewport testing would be done here)
            framework_logger.info("Step 21: Responsive layout verification across viewports")
violations.expected_code:
# Step 21: Responsive verification with actual viewport testing
            for viewport_size in [(1920, 1080), (768, 1024), (375, 667)]:
                page.set_viewport_size({"width": viewport_size[0], "height": viewport_size[1]})
                expect(print_history_page.print_history_card).to_be_visible(timeout=30000)
                framework_logger.info(f"Step 21: Verified responsive layout at {viewport_size[0]}x{viewport_size[1]}")
violations.explanation: Step 21 expected result requires responsive layout verification across Desktop, Mobile, and Tablet viewports, but the script only logs a message without performing any viewport resizing or verification. Checkpoint B05 states: No step has ONLY logging and NO action or assertion.

violations.current_code:
# ADD TO __init__ METHOD:

# Billing Cycle Period Card locators

violations.expected_code:
# ADD TO Elements INNER CLASS:

class Elements:
    # Billing Cycle Period Card locators
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    complimentary_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-progress-bar']"
    complimentary_pages_value = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-value']"
    complimentary_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-icon']"
    complimentary_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='complimentary-pages-info-message']"
    additional_pages_progress_bar = "[data-testid='additional-pages-progress-bar']"
    additional_pages_value = "[data-testid='additional-pages-value']"
    additional_pages_info_icon = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-icon']"
    additional_pages_info_message = "[data-testid='additional-pages-info-message']"
    complimentary_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"
    additional_pages_tooltip = "[role='tooltip']"

# ADD TO __init__ METHOD:

# Billing Cycle Period Card locators (accessed via .elements. namespace)
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator(self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card)
self.complimentary_pages_progress_bar = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_progress_bar)
self.complimentary_pages_value = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_value)
self.complimentary_pages_info_icon = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_info_icon)
self.complimentary_pages_info_message = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_info_message)
self.additional_pages_progress_bar = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_progress_bar)
self.additional_pages_value = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_value)
self.additional_pages_info_icon = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_info_icon)
self.additional_pages_info_message = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_info_message)
self.complimentary_pages_tooltip = page.locator(self.elements.complimentary_pages_tooltip)
self.additional_pages_tooltip = page.locator(self.elements.additional_pages_tooltip)
violations.explanation: The page_additions file defines locators as direct properties (self.X = page.locator(...)) without using the Elements inner class pattern. According to Checkpoint F04 and the framework architecture, locators should be defined in an Elements inner class as string selectors, then accessed in __init__ via page.locator(self.elements.X). This allows for the .elements. namespace access pattern used throughout the framework.

fix_details.description: Restructure to use Elements inner class pattern
fix_details.required_structure: Elements class with string selectors, then self.X = page.locator(self.elements.X)

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace every page.locator('[data-testid=...]') in the test function body with the corresponding POM reference from print_history_page. For locators defined in page_additions (plan_pause_info, complimentary_pages_progress_bar, complimentary_pages_value, complimentary_pages_info_icon, complimentary_pages_info_message, additional_pages_progress_bar, additional_pages_value, additional_pages_info_icon, additional_pages_info_message), use print_history_page.{locator_name}. For tooltips, first add them to page_additions, then reference via print_history_page.complimentary_pages_tooltip and print_history_page.additional_pages_tooltip. Use the verification methods defined in page_additions (verify_plan_pause_info_not_displayed, verify_plan_pause_info_displayed, verify_complimentary_pages_value, hover_complimentary_info_icon, verify_additional_pages_value) instead of inline locators with expect().

Add actual verification actions for steps that currently only log messages. Step 20 needs screenshot capture. Step 21 needs viewport resizing and verification. Precondition 4 needs actual pause plan implementation and verification. Step 10 needs clarification on how to specify page count in send_rtp_devicestatus().

Replace the log-only Step 21 with actual responsive testing code that resizes viewport to desktop (1920x1080), tablet (768x1024), and mobile (375x667) sizes and verifies the billing cycle card is visible at each size.

Restructure page_additions to follow the Elements inner class pattern. Define all selectors as strings in an Elements class, then in __init__ create locators using page.locator(self.elements.{selector_name}). This enables the .elements. namespace access pattern.
Remove the unused 'import time' statement from the imports section.
Add assertion after navigation to verify the print history page loaded successfully.
Consolidate multiple log lines per step into single concise log statements where appropriate.
